name: Onyx
author: Larry Back (1995) / AGP port
description: Larry Back's connection game with a capture rule (1995), published in Abstract Games #4. On a 12x12 lattice of interlocking squares and triangles (204 points including the squares' midpoints), Black connects top-bottom and White left-right. A midpoint may only be taken while its square's four corners are all empty; completing a 2-vs-2 crosscut on a square whose midpoint is empty captures the two enemy stones (two squares at once = four). Official setup pre-places four stones per side; the second player may swap after the first move (pie rule).
